title: potentilla arenaria subvar. parviflora
intro: Potentilla arenaria subvar. parviflora, also known as a dwarf or small-flowered cinquefoil, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and skin issues. The health benefits of Potentilla arenaria subvar. parviflora include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, with the dried roots and flowers being commonly used in traditional medicine.
